Project code

Sean and Dylan

2026-04-14

Univarite Data

loan <- read.csv("/Users/seandasovich/Downloads/loan_data.csv")
library(ggplot2)

loan$loan_status <- factor(loan$loan_status, levels = c(0, 1),
                           labels = c("Rejected", "Approved"))

loan$previous_loan_defaults_on_file <- factor(loan$previous_loan_defaults_on_file)
loan$person_gender <- factor(loan$person_gender)
loan$person_education <- factor(loan$person_education)
loan$person_home_ownership <- factor(loan$person_home_ownership)
loan$loan_intent <- factor(loan$loan_intent)

ggplot(loan, aes(x = person_income)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Histrogram of Income")

ggplot(loan, aes(x = credit_score)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Histrogram Credit Score")

ggplot(loan, aes(x = loan_percent_income)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  labs(title = "Histrogram of Loan Percent Income")

ggplot(loan, aes(x = loan_status)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = "Bar graph of Loan Approval Counts")

ggplot(loan, aes(x = previous_loan_defaults_on_file)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(title = " Bar Graph of Previous Loan Defaults")

## Multivariate EDA

ggplot(loan, aes(x = loan_status, y = person_income)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Income vs Loan Approval")

ggplot(loan, aes(x = loan_status, y = credit_score)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Credit Score vs Loan Approval")

ggplot(loan, aes(x = loan_status, y = loan_percent_income)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Loan Burden vs Loan Approval")

ggplot(loan, aes(x = previous_loan_defaults_on_file, fill = loan_status)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Previous Defaults vs Loan Approval")

Numeric vs loan status predictors

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(loan, aes(x = loan_status, y = person_income, fill = loan_status)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Income by Loan Status",
       x = "Loan Status", y = "Income")

ggplot(loan, aes(x = loan_status, y = credit_score, fill = loan_status)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Credit Score by Loan Status",
       x = "Loan Status", y = "Credit Score")

ggplot(loan, aes(x = loan_status, y = loan_percent_income, fill = loan_status)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Loan Percent Income by Loan Status",
       x = "Loan Status", y = "Loan Percent Income")

caterogical vs loan status

ggplot(loan, aes(x = previous_loan_defaults_on_file, fill = loan_status)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Loan Status by Previous Loan Default",
       x = "Previous Loan Default", y = "Proportion") 

ggplot(loan, aes(x = person_home_ownership, fill = loan_status)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Loan Status by Home Ownership",
       x = "Home Ownership", y = "Proportion") 

ggplot(loan, aes(x = loan_intent, fill = loan_status)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Loan Status by Loan Intent",
       x = "Loan Intent", y = "Proportion") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

Multicollinearity

numeric_vars <- loan[, c("person_age", "person_income", "loan_amnt",
                         "loan_int_rate", "credit_score", "loan_percent_income")]

cor(numeric_vars, use = "complete.obs")
##                      person_age person_income   loan_amnt loan_int_rate
## person_age           1.00000000   0.193697781 0.050749541   0.013401640
## person_income        0.19369778   1.000000000 0.242290131   0.001509828
## loan_amnt            0.05074954   0.242290131 1.000000000   0.146093082
## loan_int_rate        0.01340164   0.001509828 0.146093082   1.000000000
## credit_score         0.17843247   0.035919225 0.009074282   0.011497752
## loan_percent_income -0.04329864  -0.234176548 0.593011449   0.125209488
##                     credit_score loan_percent_income
## person_age           0.178432470         -0.04329864
## person_income        0.035919225         -0.23417655
## loan_amnt            0.009074282          0.59301145
## loan_int_rate        0.011497752          0.12520949
## credit_score         1.000000000         -0.01148310
## loan_percent_income -0.011483096          1.00000000

Logistic Regression

model1 <- glm(loan_status ~ credit_score + person_income + loan_amnt,
              data = loan, family = binomial)
## Warning: glm.fit: fitted probabilities numerically 0 or 1 occurred
summary(model1)
## 
## Call:
## glm(formula = loan_status ~ credit_score + person_income + loan_amnt, 
##     family = binomial, data = loan)
## 
## Coefficients:
##                 Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)   -3.876e-01  1.528e-01  -2.536   0.0112 *  
## credit_score  -9.418e-05  2.381e-04  -0.396   0.6925    
## person_income -2.820e-05  4.996e-07 -56.449   <2e-16 ***
## loan_amnt      1.138e-04  2.237e-06  50.873   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 47674  on 44999  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance: 42285  on 44996  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 42293
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 6
